prompt_preview:
You are a classical planning system. You are given a planning domain and a problem both in PDDL syntax.

STRICT REQUIREMENT: Your response must contain ONLY the executable plan — nothing else. No preamble, no explanation, no "We need to...", no natural-language commentary, no markdown unless it is a

message.content:
```pddl
(ingest_payment_instruction payer_entity_1)
(assign_settlement_channel_to_payment payer_entity_1 settlement_channel_ach)
(assign_validation_service_to_payment payer_entity_1 validation_service_primary)
(authorize_payment_after_validation payer_entity_1 validation_service_primary)
(evaluate_payer_route_option payer_entity_1 payer_route_option_default validation_service_primary)
(assign_manual_operator_to_payment payer_entity_1 manual_operator_1)
(operator_confirm_payer_routing payer_entity_1 payer_route_option_default manual_operator_1)
(release_manual_operator_from_payment payer_entity_1 manual_operator_1)
(release_validation_service_from_payment payer_entity_1 validation_service_primary)
(ingest_payment_instruction payee_entity_1)
(assign_settlement_channel_to_payment payee_entity_1 settlement_channel_rtp)
(assign_validation_service_to_payment payee_entity_1 validation_service_primary)
(authorize_payment_after_validation payee_entity_1 validation_service_primary)
(evaluate_payee_route_option payee_entity_1 payee_route_option_default validation_service_primary)
(assign_manual_operator_to_payment payee_entity_1 manual_operator_1)
(operator_confirm_payee_routing payee_entity_1 payee_route_option_default manual_operator_1)
(release_manual_operator_from_payment payee_entity_1 manual_operator_1)
(release_validation_service_from_payment payee_entity_1 validation_service_primary)
(compose_settlement_message payer_entity_1 payee_entity_1 payer_route_option_default payee_route_option_default settlement_message_slot_1)
(assign_validation_service_to_payment payer_entity_1 validation_service_primary)
(mark_settlement_message_ready_for_submission settlement_message_slot_1 payer_entity_1 validation_service_primary)
(finalize_payer_entity_processing_for_message payer_entity_1 settlement_message_slot_1)
(finalize_payee_entity_processing_for_message payee_entity_1 settlement_message_slot_1)
(register_retry_and_bind_operational_ticket payer_entity_1 operational_ticket_1 validation_service_primary)
(initiate_retry_for_payer_entity payer_entity_1 settlement_channel_ach operational_ticket_1)
(release_validation_service_from_payment payer_entity_1 validation_service_primary)
(assign_validation_service_to_payment payee_entity_1 validation_service_primary)
(register_retry_and_bind_operational_ticket payee_entity_1 operational_ticket_1 validation_service_primary)
(initiate_retry_for_payee_entity payee_entity_1 settlement_channel_rtp operational_ticket_1)
(release_validation_service_from_payment payee_entity_1 validation_service_primary)
(ingest_payment_instruction processing_component_1)
(assign_settlement_channel_to_payment processing_component_1 settlement_channel_correspondent)
(assign_validation_service_to_payment processing_component_1 validation_service_primary)
(authorize_payment_after_validation processing_component_1 validation_service_primary)
(consume_attachment_and_link_to_message processing_component_1 message_attachment_invoice settlement_message_slot_1)
(prepare_component_for_enrichment processing_component_1 message_attachment_invoice settlement_message_slot_1 validation_service_primary)
(assign_authorization_credential_to_component processing_component_1 authorization_credential_manager)
(assign_manual_operator_to_payment processing_component_1 manual_operator_1)
(assign_third_party_approval_to_component processing_component_1 third_party_approval_audit_team)
(start_component_authorization_flow_no_payee_attachment processing_component_1 authorization_credential_manager manual_operator_1 message_attachment_invoice settlement_message_slot_1)
(complete_component_authorization_without_policy_template processing_component_1 third_party_approval_audit_team message_attachment_invoice settlement_message_slot_1)
(release_authorization_credential_from_component processing_component_1 authorization_credential_manager)
(release_third_party_approval_from_component processing_component_1 third_party_approval_audit_team)
(release_manual_operator_from_payment processing_component_1 manual_operator_1)
(finalize_component_enrichment processing_component_1)
(register_retry_and_bind_operational_ticket processing_component_1 operational_ticket_1 validation_service_primary)
(initiate_retry_for_processing_component processing_component_1 settlement_channel_correspondent operational_ticket_1)
(release_validation_service_from_payment processing_component_1 validation_service_primary)
(ingest_payment_instruction processing_component_2)
(assign_settlement_channel_to_payment processing_component_2 settlement_channel_ach)
(assign_validation_service_to_payment processing_component_2 validation_service_primary)
(authorize_payment_after_validation processing_component_2 validation_service_primary)
(consume_attachment_and_link_to_message processing_component_2 message_attachment_remittance settlement_message_slot_1)
(prepare_component_for_enrichment processing_component_2 message_attachment_remittance settlement_message_slot_1 validation_service_primary)
(assign_authorization_credential_to_component processing_component_2 authorization_credential_manager)
(assign_manual_operator_to_payment processing_component_2 manual_operator_1)
(assign_third_party_approval_to_component processing_component_2 third_party_approval_audit_team)
(start_component_authorization_flow_no_payee_attachment processing_component_2 authorization_credential_manager manual_operator_1 message_attachment_remittance settlement_message_slot_1)
(complete_component_authorization_without_policy_template processing_component_2 third_party_approval_audit_team message_attachment_remittance settlement_message_slot_1)
(release_authorization_credential_from_component processing_component_2 authorization_credential_manager)
(release_third_party_approval_from_component processing_component_2 third_party_approval_audit_team)
(release_manual_operator_from_payment processing_component_2 manual_operator_1)
(finalize_component_enrichment processing_component_2)
(register_retry_and_bind_operational_ticket processing_component_2 operational_ticket_1 validation_service_primary)
(initiate_retry_for_processing_component processing_component_2 settlement_channel_ach operational_ticket_1)
(release_validation_service_from_payment processing_component_2 validation_service_primary)
```
message.reasoning:
The problem is a planning problem in PDDL for payments settlement with failed payment retry or repair. The goal is to get payer_entity_1, payee_entity_1, processing_component_1, and processing_component_2 all released for retry or repair.


## Investigating payment settlement  
- The task involves planning in PDDL for a payments settlement scenario.  
- It includes handling failed payments with options for retry or repair.  

## Defining release conditions  
- The goal is to ensure payer_entity_1, payee_entity_1, and processing_components_1 and 2 are all released.  
- Release is necessary for initiating retry or repair processes.

## Identifying entity conditions  
- Each entity (payer_entity_1, payee_entity_1, processing_component_1, processing_component_2) must be entity_retry_registered, entity_assigned_settlement_channel, and entity_bound_to_operational_ticket, and not already released.  
- Processing components follow similar conditions as entities for retry registration.  

## Detailing retry registration  
- To register for retry, each entity needs entity_processing_completed, validation_service allocated, and an available operational_ticket.  
- The register_retry_and_bind_operational_ticket action binds the ticket and registers retry for each entity.  

## Assessing initial state  
- Initially, no entities have assigned settlement channels, payment_ingested, or entity_authorized predicates.  
- The process likely starts from ingest_payment_instruction, progressing to processing_completed for retry release.  

## Managing resource constraints  
- Shared resources include one validation_service_primary, one manual_operator_1, and one operational_ticket_1.  
- Sequencing and allocating these resources carefully is needed for each entity’s processing.  

## Examining processing completion  
- Entity_processing_completed is achieved via actions like finalize_payer_entity_processing_for_message for payer and payee, requiring readiness and confirmation steps.  
- For processing_components, completion involves finalize_component_enrichment or finalize_component_and_mark_processing, with complex flows.

## Exploring resource allocation  
- Validation_service_primary must be allocated to each entity for retry registration, but only one is available.  
- Channels and operational_ticket can be reused after an entity is released, allowing sequential processing of all four entities.

## Planning entity processing  
- Start by ingesting all four entities (payer_entity_1, payee_entity_1, processing_component_1, processing_component_2) since none are initially ingested or released.  
- Assign settlement channels to entities, noting only three channels exist for four entities, but channels can be reused after release for sequential processing.

## Outlining processing steps  
- For processing_component_1, ingest, assign a channel, allocate validation service, and authorize payment to reach entity_authorized.  
- Further steps include assigning remediation authorizer, leveraging shared resources like validation_service_primary for sequential flows.

## Exploring component processing  
- For processing_component_2, remediation with compliance authorizer is blocked since compliance is unavailable.  
- Exploring alternative paths like enrichment or authorization for processing_component_2 to achieve processing_completed.  

## Investigating enrichment flow  
- Finalize_component_enrichment requires processing_component_authorized, no policy attached, and not finalized to reach processing_completed.  
- Achieving processing_component_authorized involves complete_component_authorization actions, needing approval initiated and various attachment conditions.  

## Analyzing message dependencies  
- Component processing often requires settlement messages enqueued, linked to attachments, and marked for submission, typically composed via payer and payee.  
- Preparing components for enrichment needs authorized status, consumed attachments, and linked messages, suggesting payer and payee processing may be prerequisite.

## Examining component authorization  
- For processing_component_2, remediation with compliance authorizer is blocked due to unavailability, unlike processing_component_1, which uses ops_lead.  
- No actions exist to assign a remediation authorizer dynamically, as "has" predicates are pre-set in initialization.  

## Evaluating alternative flows  
- Finalize_component_enrichment requires processing_component_authorized, with no bypass for authorization, limiting options for processing_component_2.  
- Authorize_payment_after_validation is the only action setting entity_authorized, requiring all entities to follow ingest, channel assignment, validation, and authorization steps.  

## Considering message dependencies  
- Preparing components for enrichment needs consumed and linked attachments, tied to enqueued and marked messages, suggesting payer and payee processing first.  
- Processing components may require completing payer and payee flows to compose messages, enabling attachment processing for components.

## Unraveling authorization loops  
- Starting component authorization flow requires processing_component_has_authorization_credential, but assigning it needs entity_authorized first, creating a catch-22.  
- Entity_authorized can only be set via authorize_payment_after_validation, suggesting components must go through validation before authorization flows.  

## Distinguishing predicates  
- Entity_authorized and processing_component_authorized are separate predicates, with entity_authorized being general and processing_component_authorized specific to components.  
- Actions like complete_component_authorization set processing_component_authorized, requiring enrichment_ready and approval initiation, while remediation uses entity_authorized.  

## Exploring remediation options  
- For processing_component_2, remediation with compliance authorizer is blocked due to unavailability, limiting options without dynamic assignment.  
- Applying remediation code is possible but separate from authorizer, suggesting no bypass for missing remediation_authorizer_compliance.

## Sequencing payer and payee processing
- Processing payer and payee requires composing messages and finalizing processing_completed, needing messages enqueued and marked first.
- Steps include ingesting entities, assigning settlement channels (e.g., ACH for payer, RTP for payee), and allocating validation services.

## Managing resource allocation
- Validation and manual operator resources are shared; releasing validation from payer enables allocation to payee.
- Actions like release_validation_service_from_payment and release_manual_operator_from_payment make resources available again.

## Evaluating routing options
- Evaluating payer and payee route options requires validation allocated, entity authorized, and no attachments, leading to route readiness.
- Operator confirmation of routing (e.g., operator_confirm_payer_routing) marks entities as ready and confirmed, using manual operator and validation.

## Handling validation dependencies
- Finalizing payer and payee processing for messages may leave validation allocated, affecting retry registration needing processing_completed and validation.
- Registering retries for payer and payee requires processing_completed and validation allocated, depending on prior allocation status.

## Exploring component specifics
- Processing components have specific message attachments (e.g., invoice for proc1, remittance for proc2), both available, but shared resources limit parallel processing.
- Only one auth_credential_manager, manual operator, third-party approver, and validation service are available, impacting sequencing.

## Handling component initialization  
- Component initialization (proc1, proc2) involves assigning credentials, operators, and third-party approvals, ensuring availability from prior releases.  
- Processes like consume and prepare set message attachments (e.g., invoice for proc1, remittance for proc2), linking multiple attachments to the same message.

## Managing resource lifecycle  
- Resources like validation, operators, and credentials are released and reassigned across payer, payee, and components, enabling sequential processing.  
- Operational tickets and settlement channels (e.g., ACH, RTP, correspondent) are freed after initiation, supporting reuse in later steps.

## Sequencing processing steps  
- Payer processing precedes payee, with validation reassigned between them for marking messages, requiring payer_entity for marking.  
- Components follow similar steps: ingest, authorize, evaluate, and finalize, with validation and operator resources released after each completion.

## Continuing resource lifecycle  
- Retry initiation for payer and payee involves binding operational tickets and releasing validation services, ensuring resource availability.  
- Components (proc1, proc2) follow similar retry patterns, using settlement channels (corr for proc1, ACH for proc2) and releasing resources post-initiation.

pddl2nl_prompt.note: autoplanbench_pddl2nl: run_save_descriptions.py --type full — шаблоны/JSON, без вызова LLM на построение промпта для сэмпла.

domain_setup.note: Файл domain_llm_usage_seedN.json не найден для section/subsection этого id (старый autoplanbench или домен не пересобирали). После run_domain_setup с обновлённым autoplanbench файл появится рядом с domain_description_seedN.json.

pipeline_usage.totals_scope_note: Per-run totals = planning + pddl2nl_prompt + nl2pddl (только tracked=true). domain_setup всегда вне totals (included_in_per_run_totals=false); цифры в блоке — для справки, агрегировать отдельно: domain_llm_usage_seed*.json + дедуп в collect_pipeline_token_usage.py.
